title: Zoro Draws His Swords
description: On pages 9–12, zoro draws his swords as while the crew sleeps after the feast, Zoro stays awake and discovers the town’s real identity as a nest of bounty hunters.
scene: Zoro Draws His Swords. While the crew sleeps after the feast, Zoro stays awake and discovers the town’s real identity as a nest of bounty hunters. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Oh dear Lord.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: HIC! That bunch sure knows how to eat and drink.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And I was only drinking sparkling barley tea...!

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Is there really even a need to stage this whole welcome party in the first place?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I mean, they're only a bunch of weak- looking brats...!

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Miss Monday.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: We should've just crushed them at the harbour.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: The town is short enough on food as it is already.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Sister A.K.A Miss MONDAY

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And it's not like we can count on getting that whale meat either.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Don't you talk that way about us!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's right! We tried our best, you know!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Now, now. Just calm down for now and take a look at this.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I've done my homework about this crew.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Flap

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: th...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Thirty million Bell!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: To judge a pirate's strength from his appearances alone is quite foolish, miss B-

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: MA- MAA-

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Miss Monday.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: them...!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: My apologies...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: But in any case... they're already out cold. This should make an excellent report for the boss.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Now go confiscate any money or treasures on their ship and tie them all up!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: If we kill them, we'll lose 30% of the bounty money.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The government would rather do public executions of criminals, after all.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Sorry but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Would you mind letting those guys sleep for a bit longer?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: They're all tired from the journey over here.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: M-MR.8! MS. MONDAY!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: One of their crew has escaped!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Why you...! Weren't you passed out cold after all that drinking just a moment ago...!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: A true swordsman,

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Never allows alcohol to take control of himself.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: So that's how it was... this place is a den of bounty hunters,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: And you take advantage of the overconfident newbie pirates who've just entered Grand Line !

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Looks like there's about a 100 of you.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: You'll be a fine opponent for me, Baroque works.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on zoro draws his swords. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
